seoTitle: How to Remove Buy and Sell Arrows in TradingView Charts
metaDescription: Buy and sell arrows cluttering your TradingView charts? Turn them off in the Trading settings tab to get a cleaner view for technical analysis.
intro: Those buy and sell arrows showing past trade executions can clutter a TradingView chart if they don't match how you like to analyze the market. This guide shows where to find the setting that controls them and how to switch them off. It takes just a couple of clicks once you know which menu to open.

steps.heading: Open your chart settings
steps.body: With your TradingView chart open, click the settings option near the top of the chart interface to bring up the display preferences window.

steps.heading: Go to the Trading tab
steps.body: Inside settings, find the tab related to trading or order execution - its exact label can shift slightly between TradingView updates.

steps.heading: Uncheck executions and execution labels
steps.body: Scroll to the executions section and uncheck both the executions and execution labels boxes, since these control whether the arrows and their text appear.

steps.heading: Confirm the change
steps.body: Click OK to save the settings, and the buy and sell arrows should disappear from your chart immediately.

faq.question: Where do I find the option to remove buy and sell arrows on TradingView?
faq.answer: It's inside the chart settings window, under the Trading tab, where you'll find the executions and execution labels checkboxes.

faq.question: Will removing the arrows delete my trade history?
faq.answer: No, unchecking executions only hides the visual markers on the chart - it doesn't affect your actual trade records or account history.

faq.question: Does this work on TradingView mobile as well as desktop?
faq.answer: The settings menu is structured a bit differently on mobile, but a similar executions toggle is available to hide the arrows there too.

faq.question: Can I turn the arrows back on later if I change my mind?
faq.answer: Yes, just revisit the same Trading tab in chart settings and recheck the executions and execution labels boxes to bring them back.
